The issue lies in the way the function h:
Masking the Left Operand
The function can be modified to mask the left operand with 0xffffffff before performing the unsigned right shift operation. This will ensure that the left operand is treated as an unsigned 32-bit integer, and the correct result will be returned.

```java
case Token.URSH:
    result = (lvalInt & 0xffffffff) >>> rvalInt;
    break;
```

Using a Long Literal
The function can be modified to use a long literal to represent the left operand when performing the unsigned right shift operation. This will ensure that the left operand is treated as a 64-bit integer, and the correct result will be returned.

```java
case Token.URSH:
    result = (long) lvalInt >>> rvalInt;
    break;
```
